2026-04-21T15:47:29.7968332Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7968879Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7969308Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7969696Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7970009Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7970437Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7970599Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7970781Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7970955Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7971190Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7971395Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7971690Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T15:47:29.7971956Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-21T15:52:45.7984160Z - The 12.0.0 release of `Azure.Search.Documents` added `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` (agentic retrieval against knowledge bases) as its headline feature, but the README was never updated.

2026-04-21T15:52:49.5644751Z [2026-04-21T15:47:32Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 6468b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

The affected package `Azure.Search.Documents` 12.0.0 (released 2026-04-14) introduced `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` for agentic retrieval against knowledge bases — a major new feature — but the README has never been updated to document it.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The README has **no section** documenting `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` or the Knowledge Base Retrieval feature, despite it being the headline new addition in version 12.0.0.\n- A complete test-backed sample file already exists at `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` with seven `#region Snippet:` regions ready for inclusion, but none are referenced in the README.\n- The Examples table-of-contents in the README does not list Knowledge Base Retrieval.\n- The Key Concepts section has no description of knowledge bases, knowledge sources, or agentic retrieval.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File:** `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/README.md` — **edit**\n\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the existing Vector search sub-section, before Thread safety), add a new sub-section:\n\n```markdown\n#### Knowledge base retrieval\n\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` provides agentic retrieval operations against Azure AI Search knowledge bases. A knowledge base is a managed collection of knowledge sources (search indexes, Azure Blobs, web content, OneLake) with an associated Azure OpenAI model that can synthesize responses to natural-language intents. Use `KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` to query the knowledge base with semantic intents and receive synthesized answers with cited references.\n```\n\n2. In the **Examples** table-of-contents block, add a new bullet after `* [Async APIs](#async-apis)`:\n\n```markdown\n* [Knowledge base retrieval](#knowledge-base-retrieval)\n```\n\n3. After the `### Async APIs` section (and before `### Authenticate in a National Cloud`), add a new section:\n\n````markdown\n### Knowledge base retrieval\n\n`KnowledgeBaseRetrievalClient` enables agentic retrieval from an Azure AI Search knowledge base. First, add the required using directives:\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces\n```\n\nThen create a client and issue a retrieval request with a natural-language intent:\n\n```C# Snippet:Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve\n```\n````\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nThe snippet tool will populate the placeholder blocks above from the existing test file. No new test code needs to be written — `Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` already contains the required regions.\n\n| Snippet name | Test file location | Region start line |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Namespaces` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 10 |\n| `Azure_Search_Documents_Tests_Samples_Sample10_KnowledgeBase_Retrieve` | `sdk/search/Azure.Search.Documents/tests/Samples/Sample10_KnowledgeBaseRetrieval.cs` | 39 |\n\nInsert the placeholder blocks exactly as shown in Step 1 above. `Update-Snippets.ps1` will replace the content between the fences.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README already contains all six required top-level sections. 2026-04-21T15:52:49.6837973Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-21T15:52:49.6838182Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-21T15:52:49.6838524Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-04-21T15:52:49.6838789Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
